---
title: "Advanced_Git"
subtitle: "Terminal-Based Git for Rehabilitation Science Researchers"
---

## Welcome

This course teaches Git entirely through the command line — no graphical interfaces,
no point-and-click. You will start from zero terminal experience and finish with
the skills to manage, share, and document your research code professionally.

**No prerequisites required.** If you can open a browser, you can start here.

## Why Learn Git via the Terminal?

Rehabilitation science increasingly demands reproducible, documented workflows.
The terminal gives you that — and more:

- **Reproducibility**: Every command you run can be copied, saved, and shared exactly
- **Documentation**: Your terminal history *is* your audit trail
- **Portability**: Terminal commands work identically on every machine, every server,
  every collaborator's computer
- **Understanding**: The terminal shows you what Git is actually doing — no black boxes

GUI tools hide the mechanics that matter when things go wrong. The terminal puts
you in control.

## What You Will Learn

After completing this course, you will be able to:

1. Navigate file systems and run commands in the terminal
2. Understand Git's internal structure (the `.git` repository)
3. Explain Git's conceptual model: snapshots, commits, branches, HEAD
4. Perform all basic Git operations from the command line
5. Create and manage branches entirely in the terminal
6. Resolve merge conflicts using terminal text editors
7. Clean commit history with interactive rebase
8. Collaborate via terminal-based workflows
9. Protect sensitive research data with proper `.gitignore` configuration
10. Troubleshoot Git problems by understanding what Git does internally
11. Document research workflows through commit history

## Learning Path

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A([Start Here]) --> B[Module 00\nTerminal Fundamentals]
    B --> C[Module 01\nGit Concepts]
    C --> D[Module 02\nBasic Workflow]
    D --> E[Module 03\nRemote Repositories]
    E --> F[Module 04\nBranching]
    F --> G[Module 05\nMerging & Conflicts]
    G --> H{Advanced\nTopics}
    H --> I[Module 06\nRebase]
    H --> J[Module 07\nAdvanced Ops]
    H --> K[Module 08\nData Management]
    K --> L[Module 09\nCollaboration]
    L --> M[Module 10\nTroubleshooting]
    M --> N[Module 11\nTool Integration]
    N --> O([Module 12\nProfessional Repos])

    style A fill:#50fa7b,color:#000
    style O fill:#50fa7b,color:#000
    style K fill:#ff5555,color:#fff
```

::: {.callout-important}
## Before Module 03 (Remotes): Read Module 08 First
Module 08 covers what **must not** go into a repository (participant data, credentials,
large files). Read the Data Safety section at the end of **Module 02** before pushing
to any remote. Module 08 provides the full treatment.
:::

## Before You Begin: Set Up Your Environment

Choose your operating system and follow the setup guide:

::: {.panel-tabset}
## macOS
[macOS Setup Guide →](resources/setup-guides/macos.qmd)

Install Git via Homebrew, configure your terminal, and set up SSH keys.
Estimated time: 15 minutes.

## Linux
[Linux Setup Guide →](resources/setup-guides/linux.qmd)

Install Git via your package manager, confirm your bash/zsh environment,
and set up SSH keys. Estimated time: 10 minutes.

## Windows
[Windows Setup Guide →](resources/setup-guides/windows.qmd)

Install Git for Windows (Git Bash) or set up WSL2. Both paths are documented.
Estimated time: 20–30 minutes.
:::

## How to Use This Course

Each module follows the same structure:

1. **Read the concept** — understand what and why before any commands
2. **Study the annotated examples** — every command shows its exact output
3. **Work through the exercises** — hands-on practice with a real research project
4. **Check your output** — collapse the "Expected Output" to verify your results

**This course uses a running example**: a fictional grip-strength rehabilitation study
(`rehab-research/`). You will build this repository from scratch in Module 00 and
carry it through the entire course.

## Quick Navigation

| I want to… | Go to… |
|------------|--------|
| Open a terminal for the first time | [Module 00](modules/00-terminal-fundamentals.qmd) |
| Understand what Git actually does | [Module 01](modules/01-git-concepts.qmd) |
| Start committing my research scripts | [Module 02](modules/02-basic-workflow.qmd) |
| Push my work to GitHub | [Module 03](modules/03-remotes.qmd) |
| Protect my data files from Git | [Module 08](modules/08-data-management.qmd) |
| Fix a mistake I just made | [Module 10](modules/10-troubleshooting.qmd) |
| Look up a command I forgot | [Command Index](reference/command-index.qmd) |
| Decode an error message | [Error Messages](reference/error-messages.qmd) |
